Her eyes followed the way Aurora had spoken to Ethan, the ease with which she handled the conversation, and the soft smile that played on her lips as she tucked the business card away.

"How smug," Julia thought, her lips curling into a smirk.

She found it amusing that Aurora, so calm and composed, had no idea what was brewing behind the scenes.

Julia’s gaze briefly flickered between Aurora and the rest of the guests, noting how effortlessly Aurora fit into this world now, like she belonged there.

"But that wouldn’t last. Not after tonight," Julia mused.

With a final glance at Aurora, Julia turned and spotted her next target—Ben.

He was lounging in a plush corner of the rooftop, surrounded by actresses in tight, revealing gowns, their hands draped over him like accessories.

The table before them was cluttered with half-empty bottles of expensive alcohol and a hookah pipe, with smoke curling lazily in the air.

Ben’s presence was magnetic, dangerous, and clearly adored by the women around him, who giggled and touched his chest flirtatiously.

Julia narrowed her eyes and approached, determined.

She leaned over the table slightly, her voice cutting through the lively chatter. "Ben, I need to speak with you. Privately."

Ben raised an eyebrow, shifting his arm from one of the actresses draped over his shoulder.

His predatory gaze fell on Julia, and his smirk deepened. "Unless you’re willing to replace one of these beauties, I’m not interested in any conversation." He nodded toward the women surrounding him, who continued to flirt, their hands still resting on him.

Julia fought the urge to scowl, knowing full well how dangerous Ben could be, both in and out of the industry.

She crossed her arms instead, keeping her voice steady. "Do you know Aurora Grey?"

Ben’s expression shifted ever so slightly. His interest piqued. "You mean that gorgeous curvy beauty?" He raised an eyebrow.

Julia smiled inwardly, her plan moving into place.

"Yes, her. Look at her," Julia said, pointing across the rooftop to where Aurora stood alone.

Ben followed her gaze, his eyes locking on Aurora.

A slow, lustful smile spread across his face as he licked his lips.

"Now, that’s a sight," he murmured before dismissing the women around him with a wave of his hand.

The actresses looked annoyed, but they had no choice but to leave, casting cold glances at Julia as they slinked away.

Ben leaned forward, resting his elbows on his knees, his gaze still fixated on Aurora. "So, what’s the deal, Julia?"

Julia sat beside him, her smile growing. "I’ll give her to you, Ben. Aurora will willingly go to your room. You can do whatever you want with her. Meanwhile, I’ll take care of Lucas."

Ben smirked, his eyes gleaming with anticipation, but the expression faltered for a moment. "And what if Aurora resists? She doesn’t exactly seem like the type who’d just go along with something like that."

Julia flicked her hair back, exuding confidence. "Aurora won’t have a choice. She’ll be forced to accept. My men will ensure that if she even looks at another man, all she’ll think about is getting laid. Trust me, Ben, you won’t have to worry."

Ben’s grin returned, wicked and approving. "I like the sound of that."

Inwardly, Julia celebrated her success.

Everything was falling into place.

"Yes, it’s all coming together." She mused over her plan.

Lucas was already in the hotel, drugged by her men.

Cameras had been set up in his room, and Ben’s, ready to capture every moment.

Videos of her with Lucas would be leaked, forcing him to finally realize she was the treasure he had ignored for too long.

Aurora, meanwhile, would be ruined—her reputation shattered when her intimate encounter with Ben went public.

In Julia’s mind, it was perfect. Lucas would have no choice but to marry her, and Aurora would be left with nothing.

chapters.title: Chapter 66: Aurora’s Heat
chapters.content:
Chapter 66: Aurora’s Heat

Aurora’s glass lay empty, the sparkling water drained in mere minutes.

The evening’s din surrounded her – laughter, clinking glasses, and muted conversations – yet she felt somewhat disconnected, as if floating above the scene.

Something nagged at her, an unsettling sense of unease.

Then, without warning, a wave of heat swept through her body. Her skin prickled, her breath caught in short gasps.

The dizziness hit her hard, and she instinctively reached for a railing to steady herself.

She needed to leave—right now.

Something wasn’t right.

Her mind felt clouded, unable to process clearly, but her instincts screamed to get out of the rooftop and away from whatever was happening.

With shaky legs, she made her way toward the exit, her fingers fumbling uselessly over the elevator buttons.

Unable to wait for the lift, she took the stairs, gripping the railing as she descended slowly, each step feeling heavier than the last.

Her thoughts swirled—What’s happening to me?—as her vision blurred, and all she could focus on was the overwhelming heat consuming her.

Halfway down the stairs, she stopped, panting.

And then, from the shadows, Julia stepped into view. Her smirk was sharp and cutting, a cruel glint in her eyes as she watched Aurora’s struggle.

Aurora, still burning with the unnatural heat coursing through her veins, glared at Julia, suspicion dawning on her.

"What... did you do to me?" Aurora’s voice came out strained.

Julia took a step closer, her smile widening. "By tomorrow morning, it’ll be you asking yourself, ’What have I done?’"

Julia tilted her head, eyes gleaming with satisfaction. "This is what you get for pouring wastewater on me, Aurora."

Aurora’s heart sank as she realized that Julia had orchestrated this. But before she could even attempt to move or fight back, Julia snapped her fingers, and a large, rugged man appeared from behind her.

Aurora tried to resist as he approached, but her body betrayed her, the dizziness making her movements sluggish and uncoordinated.

The man grabbed her roughly by the arm, dragging her down the remaining stairs.

Aurora struggled, but the heat, the dizziness—it was all too much.

She barely registered the door to room 203 as the man pushed her inside, slamming the door shut behind her and locking it with a sharp click.

Disoriented, Aurora stumbled to the door, banging her fists weakly against it.

"Let me out!" she cried, but the response was only silence.

The heat was intensifying now, crawling over her skin like flames.

She turned, backing away from the door just as it opened.

The room was dimly lit, casting long shadows on the walls.

A figure stepped inside, and the door closed behind them with a soft thud.

Aurora’s breath caught in her throat as the silhouette of a man became clearer, slowly advancing toward her.

Panic surged, but so did the strange desire that had been overtaking her thoughts.

She tried to fight it—tried to regain control—but it was slipping away.

Her legs hit the bed, and she stumbled backwards, reaching out for the bedside lamp in a desperate attempt to defend herself.

The man moved faster, grabbing her wrist before she could swing it, and in one swift motion, he turned on the lamp.

The room flooded with light, and Aurora’s fear evaporated the moment she saw his face.

"Lucas?" she whispered, her voice shaky but filled with relief.

Lucas stood there, confusion written across his face.

"What’s going on? Why are you in my hotel room?" he asked, his tone sharp with concern.

Aurora opened her mouth to respond, but the words wouldn’t come.

Her body felt impossibly light, and the heat—oh, the heat—was all she could focus on.

She touched her forehead, trying to shake it off, trying to fight the pull, but it was overwhelming.

Lucas stepped closer, his brows furrowed as he took in her disoriented state.

"Aurora, what’s wrong?" he asked softly, his voice laced with worry.

He leaned in, and that’s when he smelled it.

She didn’t smell like herself—there was something different, something wild.

His eyes widened in sudden realization, his heart pounding as the truth hit him. "Aurora..."

Before Lucas could say another word, Aurora closed the distance between them, her lips crashing against his.

"Don’t speak," she whispered against his mouth, her breath hot and urgent. "I need this."

It was raw, unrestrained, her desire igniting a fire between them that neither could control.

Her body pressed against his, demanding, her need so fierce it overwhelmed him.

He stiffened for a moment, caught off guard, but the scent—the unmistakable scent of her heat—washed over him, drowning out everything else.

It consumed him, seeping into every part of him until he couldn’t think, couldn’t breathe—he could only want.

His arms instinctively wrapped around her, pulling her closer, tighter, until there was no space left between them.

His body reacted before his mind had a chance to catch up, driven by something wild, something that spoke to the core of who he was.

Aurora pulled back slightly, her lips swollen from the kiss, her breath ragged.

Her eyes, dark with desire, locked onto his, a teasing smile curving her lips.

"Careful," she whispered, her voice low and sultry, every word dripping with need. "I’m more fragile than I used to be."

Lucas’s gaze softened, the reminder of her pregnancy grounding him for a brief moment.

His breath hitched as he absorbed her words.

"I know," he said, his voice barely above a whisper. "I won’t hurt you."

But even then, the desire between them was impossible to ignore.

His hand cupped her cheek, his thumb brushing over her soft skin, before he leaned in to kiss her again.

This time, it was slower, more deliberate, as if he was savouring every moment, every taste of her.

chapters.title: Chapter 69: Security Hub
chapters.content:
Chapter 69: Security Hub

"How did you—" Julia stammered, scrambling to sit up, the blanket hastily pulled up to cover her chest.

She glared at Ben, her voice sharp and accusing. "How did you get here?!"

Ben raised an eyebrow, his voice calm but teasing. "What do you mean? I should be asking you how you got here."

He stood up, letting the blanket slip from his waist.

Julia’s eyes widened in shock as she caught a glimpse of his bare length, and she immediately let out a scream.

She shut her eyes and blindly threw the cover at him, her voice frantic.

"Cover yourself up! Immediately!"

Ben shrugged, unaffected by her reaction. "Relax," he said casually. "You’re over-exaggerating."

Her eyes stayed shut, her hand still outstretched, waiting for him to take the cover.

With a roll of his eyes, Ben picked up the blanket and draped it over himself.

"You know," he continued, his voice taking on a mocking edge, "I remember when you were still in college, working as a pole dancer. Back then, you’d kneel at my feet and—"

"Shut up!" Julia’s eyes flew open, fury blazing in them.

Before she could react, Ben was in front of her, gripping her neck with one strong hand.

His voice dropped dangerously low. "Don’t you ever yell at me again," he said, his face inches from hers.

Julia’s breath caught in her throat, her heart racing in fear.

She quickly nodded, her confidence dissolving under his iron grip.

Satisfied, Ben let her go, watching as she fell back onto the bed, gasping.

He turned and walked toward the bathroom, the sound of his steps heavy as his anger still simmered.

Julia sat there, furious, her pulse pounding in her ears.

She rose quickly, her movements stiff as she made her way to the bedside lamp.

Her hand trembled slightly as she ripped the small camera hidden on it and threw it across the room with a frustrated yell.

Then, Julia hurriedly gathered her scattered clothes from the floor, her fingers trembling as she slipped into her underwear and quickly tugged on her dress.

Her mind raced, but her movements were swift, fastening buttons and adjusting straps with urgency.

Once fully dressed, she didn’t spare a glance back at Ben.

Instead, she stormed out of the room, slamming the door behind her.

She flung open the door of the adjacent room, where the men she’d hired were stationed.

The hotel room had been transformed into a makeshift security hub.

Large monitors lined the walls, displaying various feeds from the hotel’s security cameras.

The men, rough-looking and burly, sat casually in front of the screens, their attention divided between the footage and their conversation.

When Julia entered, they almost burst into laughter.

Her face flushed with anger as she realized what they had likely seen.

She clenched her fists, her mind flashing back to her encounter with Ben.

Of course, they’d seen everything.

She gritted her teeth, reminding herself that she’d had far worse experiences during her college years.

This was nothing compared to that.

She straightened her back and shot the men a withering glare.

Their smirks faded, and they quickly sat up, their amusement subdued by her obvious anger.

"What happened?" she demanded, her voice cold. "What did you do wrong?" She paused, her eyes narrowing. "You remember what I said before I hired you. No success, no pay."

One of the men, taller and more intimidating than the rest, stood up.

His broad shoulders towered over her as he stepped closer. "We did our job right," he said slowly, his voice dripping with menace.

Julia’s heart skipped a beat, and she involuntarily took a step back.

His presence was overwhelming, and she could feel the agreement of the other men without even looking.

But she quickly crossed her arms, forcing herself to stand her ground.

"Then explain to me," she said, her voice slightly wavering but still firm, "why Ben ended up with me instead of Aurora."

The leader glanced back at his men, who nodded in agreement with him. "We put Aurora in room 203 - Ben’s room, just like you asked. Locked her in."

He frowned. "But somehow, another man got in there, and Ben ended up going to room 204—Lucas’s room." His eyes flicked toward her. "The two men went to the wrong rooms."

Julia slapped her forehead in frustration, her anger bubbling over.

"Unbelievable!" she muttered, her plan falling apart piece by piece.

But then an idea sparked in her mind.

She wasn’t completely out of options.

Aurora was still in that room 203, likely with another man.

If Julia couldn’t get Lucas to fall into her trap, she could still destroy Aurora’s reputation with the footage.

"Play the footage from Aurora’s room," Julia demanded, a smirk tugging at her lips.

The men, clearly uncomfortable with her request, exchanged uneasy glances but complied.

One of them tapped a few buttons on the remote, and the screen flickered to life, displaying the live feed from room 203.

In her mind, Julia silently gloated.

"Fate is against you, Aurora. I’ll still ruin you, no matter what."

The footage started, showing Aurora backing away from the door as it opened.

A shadowy figure entered the room, but the dim lighting made it impossible to see his face.

As Aurora moved toward the bed lamp to strike it against the figure, the camera hidden on the lamp fell to the floor, its screen turning to static as it shattered.

"Aish!" Julia screamed, her face contorting in rage.

Every last piece of her plan was crumbling before her eyes.

No Lucas!

No damning footage of Aurora!

She grit her teeth, her fury boiling over.

Without another word, she stormed out of the room.

"Hey! Where are you going?" one of the men called after her. "You haven’t paid us!"

Julia ignored them, her anger propelling her forward. She marched down the hall, her destination clear in her mind.

Room 203.

She clenched her fists, her footsteps quick and heavy.

"I have to find out who Aurora slept with. I’ll expose her to Lucas. I’ll make sure she’s ruined."

Chapter 70: Strong Bond

She closed them again, rubbing her forehead as if trying to reset her mind.

Once dressed, she stepped out, feeling the soft fabric glide against her skin.

He offered them to her with a grin.

"You love heels, but maybe we can go a bit lower this time, don’t you think?"

Aurora raised a brow. "I don’t think so. I’m perfectly fine with my usual ones. These will just make me look short."

Lucas, ever the tease, picked up one of the heels and pretended to put it on his own foot. "I’ll show you how comfortable they are."

Aurora laughed, shaking her head. "Lucas, stop! You’re ridiculous."

He grinned, unfazed. "Hey, I’m serious. I’ll wear them if you do."

Rolling her eyes with a playful grin, she gave in. "Fine, but only because I love you."

"There," he said, looking up at her with admiration. "Perfect."

"Thanks," she replied, feeling a mix of warmth and excitement.

Lucas took a step closer, a soft smile on his face. "And thanks for everything you did with the evidence. It really made a difference."

Aurora felt her heart swell with pride. "I just did what I had to do."

He nodded, the warmth in his gaze reassuring. "Still, it helped. I’m glad you’re by my side."

She smiled softly, touched by his sincerity. "I’d do it all over again for you, Lucas."

He leaned in closer, his gaze intense. "You mean a lot to me, you know that?"

Aurora felt her cheeks warm again, this time from a mix of emotions. "You mean a lot to me, too."

Lucas smiled, pulling her into a gentle kiss. Aurora melted against him, feeling safe and cherished.

"Now that’s more like it," he said, stepping back to admire her. "Just a little more height, and you look absolutely amazing."

"Okay, fine," she replied playfully, twirling slightly to show off the heels. "But I still feel like I could easily trip in these."

"Don’t worry," Lucas said, taking her hand. "I’ll catch you if you do."

With a smile, Aurora squeezed his hand and they approached the door.

Lucas reached for the doorknob and opened it for her.

Just as the door swung open, they were met with a surprise.

She blinked in surprise as the door opened, her expression shifting from confusion to a smirk when her gaze landed on Aurora.

"Well, well, if it isn’t my favorite little rabbit," Julia drawled, tilting her head slightly, her tone dripping with mock sweetness.

But her smirk faltered as her eyes flicked to Lucas, who stood close behind Aurora.

For a moment, she seemed to tremble, caught off guard.

In her mind, questions raced.

Lucas? The one who entered Ben’s room? But how? He was drugged, wasn’t he?

This realization made her uncomfortable, and for a moment, anger flashed across her face.

"Oh, you’re here, Julia," Lucas said, trying to sound relaxed but betraying a hint of nervousness.

Aurora stepped forward, her annoyance evident. "I’ll be leaving first," she stated, clearly displeased at the unexpected encounter.

As Aurora moved past Julia, Lucas watched her go, a frown knitting his brows.

He could sense Aurora’s irritation, particularly at Julia’s presence.

But as Aurora walked further away, a sly smirk played on her lips, one that Julia failed to catch in her moment of surprise.

What was Aurora up to?

Once Aurora was out of earshot, a rush of satisfaction surged through her.

Julia might have tampered with her drink in an attempt to split her and Lucas, but here she was, firmly with him.

The thought of Julia’s sabotage only bolstered Aurora’s confidence. She had won Lucas’s heart, and their bond felt stronger than ever.

chapters.title: Chapter 71: Good Terms
chapters.content:
Chapter 71: Good Terms

Back at the doorway of room 203, Lucas noticed Julia’s clenched fists as she stared at Aurora’s retreating figure.

His gaze shifted from Aurora to Julia. "You and Aurora don’t seem to be on good terms," he commented, breaking the silence.

Julia turned to face him, her expression darkening.

"How could we be on good terms?" she snapped. "Have you already forgotten everything she did to me— to us? Aurora is the reason I had to leave this city in the first place. The torment she put me through, the schemes... She destroyed our happy relationship, Lucas!"

She took a step closer to him, her voice turning sharp. "She’s nothing but a gold digger, a manipulative pest. Don’t tell me you’ve forgotten her devious plan to get a share of your wealth."

As Julia moved even closer, Lucas instinctively raised his hand, blocking her approach. His outstretched arm stopped her in her tracks.

The gesture shocked Julia, her eyes widening in disbelief.

"You really believe that, Julia?" Lucas’s voice was calm but firm. He looked directly at her, his gaze unwavering. "Actually, now that I think about it, I believe your so-called evidence was false."

Julia’s jaw dropped, too stunned to respond immediately. "What?" she stammered.

"You heard me," Lucas said, his tone confident.

Julia’s face paled as she tried to recover. "Why would you say that? I would never lie to you!"

"Well," Lucas continued, "many things suggest that the evidence you gave me was fake. You claimed Aurora faked a pregnancy to get my wealth, but in reality, she’s the daughter of a wealthy Alpha King. She has six brothers, all of whom are wealthier than most could dream. Why would she need my money, which is a fraction of what her family owns?"

Julia stood speechless, unable to defend herself. She knew the truth—deep down, she had fabricated everything. She hadn’t left the city because of Aurora’s supposed torment but for her own selfish reasons.

Lucas took a step back from her, his expression now one of disappointment. "I doubt your words now, Julia," he said, shaking his head. "You no longer seem like the honest person I thought you were."

With that, he turned and began to walk away.

Panic surged through Julia as she watched him go.

"No," she thought. "He can’t be serious."

She ran after him, catching up and grabbing his hand to stop him. "Lucas, wait! You can’t just believe Aurora because you found out about her family. She’s manipulating you. She must be twisting everything to make you doubt me."

Lucas stopped, turning slowly to face her.

With a sharp motion, he pulled his hand away from hers. "Aurora hasn’t said anything to me about the past. Not a word about you." His voice was filled with quiet anger now. "She hasn’t tried to manipulate me. All she’s done is remind me of how much she sacrificed for our love—how much she gave up for us. Do you even know that she was disowned by her family because of me?"

Julia gasped, her mind racing. She didn’t have any knowledge about Aurora being disowned.

Lucas’s eyes flashed with emotion. "If Aurora didn’t love me, she wouldn’t have left her family, her wealth, her status, for me. I just found out yesterday that she was disowned by the Grey family for our marriage, and the weight of that hit me like nothing else." His voice softened briefly. "I couldn’t even sip the drink I ordered when I heard that."

Julia clenched her fists, seething inwardly, but she remained silent, listening as Lucas continued.

"Just knowing what she sacrificed for us," he said, "makes me realize how much she truly loved me. How much she must have hurt when she found out I was in love with someone else— you."

Julia opened her mouth to speak, but Lucas cut her off. "But none of that matters anymore," he said, his tone final. "I don’t want to think about the past. Whether Aurora did wrong by you or not, I’m focused on how I’m going to reclaim her. I don’t have room in my mind for the past. So, push it aside, Julia. Move on."

Julia stood frozen, his words striking deeper than she expected.

"We’re sorry! We’ll do anything, just don’t hurt us anymore!"

Aurora listened to their pathetic groveling without a flicker of emotion.

Their cries were nothing new to her.

With a slight wave of her hand, she addressed Derek. "Remove their blindfolds."

Derek, standing by her side, gave a sharp nod.

He moved swiftly, pulling off the blindfolds one by one.

As their eyes adjusted to the light, the men squinted, still kneeling as their hands were tied behind their backs.

Aurora’s gaze lingered on one particular man, her lips curling into a cold, knowing smirk.

"Ah, so it’s him."

The same rugged-looking man who had once dragged her to that room, following Julia’s orders.

His face was unmistakable, though now bruised and pale with fear.

She set down her teacup with a soft clink, uncrossing her legs and standing up.

Slowly, she approached the group of men, her arms folding across her chest as she stopped directly in front of the rugged man.

"Well, well," she began, her voice low but laced with menace. "The world is a small place, isn’t it? Look who we have here."

She leaned down slightly, her eyes locking with his. "We meet again."

The man’s body trembled visibly under her gaze. His mouth opened, but the words stuck in his throat.

He knew exactly who she was—Julia had sent them to work against her, and now, here he was, completely at her mercy.

"Why... why are you doing this to us?" he finally stammered, his voice shaking. "You—"

He glanced fearfully at Derek before continuing, "You ordered him to beat us up, to torture us, but it’s that bitch who should be punished, not us!"

Aurora’s expression didn’t change, but she gave a small nod, understanding immediately who he was referring to.

"Ah, Julia," she said softly, her voice almost amused. "That’s precisely why you’re here."

She took a step closer, her presence looming over him. "Because you’re going to make her feel exactly what you just felt."

chapters.title: Chapter 72: Lavish Salon
chapters.content:
Chapter 72: Lavish Salon

In the lavish salon, Julia sat comfortably in one of the plush chairs, flipping her perfectly styled hair back with an air of superiority.

Her friends surrounded her, all listening intently as she boasted loudly about her latest purchase.

"Ladies, you won’t believe this," Julia said with a smug smile. "This wig? One million dollars. Custom-made. Imported from the finest sources. There’s nothing like it." She twirled a strand of the expensive wig between her fingers as her friends oohed and ahhed in amazement.

"Oh, Julia! You always have the best taste," one of her friends, Lizzy, gushed. "No one does luxury like you!"

"Of course," another chimed in. "I mean, look at her! She’s always dripping in wealth."

Julia soaked up the compliments like a sponge, smiling brightly. "Well, what can I say? Some of us were just born to live this way."

While the others showered her with praises, Sarah, seated quietly in the corner, could barely contain her irritation.

She had seen Julia pull this act before, and it left a sour taste in her mouth.

Sarah’s mind wandered back to the boutique incident not too long ago.

Julia had boasted about buying an expensive dress, making sure everyone heard her, only for her credit card to be declined at the counter.

The embarrassment Sarah felt that day still lingered, and she had no doubt that Julia was simply full of empty words today as well.

"Boasting in vain," Sarah thought bitterly.

She absentmindedly scrolled through her phone, trying to tune out Julia’s incessant bragging.

As she swiped through social media, a bold headline caught her eye.

"Bombshell Scandal: Beloved Star’s Personal Life Exposed."

Intrigued, Sarah tapped on the headline, and it led her to an article. Her eyes widened as she read through it, realizing it was all about Julia Mayhem and a scandalous affair.

The article detailed shocking claims about Julia’s personal life, exposing intimate details that Sarah could barely believe.

Then she saw it—a video was attached to the article.

Without thinking, Sarah let out a startled scream. "Julia!"

Julia turned to her sharply, narrowing her eyes. "Why are you screaming like some irresponsible woman, Sarah?" she snapped. "Honestly, if you ever want to be taken seriously, especially if you dream of becoming an A-list actress like me, you need to start acting more modest." Her tone was dripping with disdain.

Sarah rolled her eyes and hissed under her breath, pushing her phone toward Julia. "Just take a look at this."

Annoyed but curious, Julia snatched the phone from Sarah’s hand.

She barely glanced at the article, dismissing it as another silly gossip piece. "You waste my time with these trivial articles—"

But her words cut off as she tapped the video attached to the article.

The screen lit up, and as it played, Julia’s face turned pale.

Her breath hitched as she saw exactly what was playing out. It was the video of what had happened between her and Ben the other night.

The very affair she thought was hidden had now been made public in the worst way possible.

Julia let out a loud, horrified scream, much louder than Sarah’s earlier cry.

Her friends, startled, looked around in confusion, but Sarah, standing behind her, couldn’t help but smirk, hiding her laughter at Julia’s obvious distress.

Julia’s mind raced, her heart pounding.

"Who did this?"

She struggled to think of anyone who could’ve leaked such a damning video.

Then, it clicked.

The rugged men. The ones she had hired.

They had been responsible for the videos, and now it seemed they had betrayed her.

Seething in anger, Julia stood up abruptly, her face twisted in fury.

She barely heard her friends’ confused questions as she hurriedly stormed out of the salon.

Once outside, she waved down a cab and barked out the address of the club.

She needed answers, and she needed them now.

---

Inside the cab, Julia clenched her fists, her mind consumed with thoughts of revenge.

Whoever had done this would pay dearly.

Once the cab halted, Julia walked out.

Julia stormed into the dimly lit club, her heels clicking loudly against the floor as she made a beeline for the rugged man sitting at a corner table.

The neon lights flickered overhead, casting strange shadows across the room.

The man, tall and burly with a scruffy beard, was the leader of the group she had hired.

He sat there, casually sipping from a glass of whiskey, oblivious—or perhaps indifferent—to the fury that was fast approaching him.

Without a second thought, Julia stomped up to him and slammed her designer bag onto the table with a loud thud.

Her eyes blazed with anger as she leaned forward, fists clenched.

"What the hell is the meaning of this?" she yelled, her voice sharp enough to turn a few heads around them.

But the man didn’t flinch.

He took another sip of his drink, his eyes lazily lifting to meet hers as if her presence was barely worth acknowledging.

Julia’s nostrils flared at his silence.

"I’m talking to you!" she snapped, slamming her hand onto the table. "Aren’t you the one I hired? Why would you do this? What do you think you’re playing at?"

The rugged man set his glass down and finally leaned back in his chair, a slow, lazy smile creeping onto his lips.

"I did what I had to do," he said simply, his voice low and gruff, but calm.

Julia’s fury only intensified at his nonchalant response.

She clenched her fists so tightly her knuckles turned white. "What do you mean you did what you had to do? Does that mean you’re the one behind those videos? You leaked them?!"

The man shrugged, unfazed by her outburst. "Maybe. Maybe not."

"That’s not an answer!" Julia nearly screamed. "If you’re behind those videos, you’ll take them down. Right now!" She pointed a finger at him, her demand clear and forceful.

The man chuckled darkly and reached for his drink again, taking another slow sip before setting it back down.

"Take them down?" He looked up at her with a smirk. "You think it’s that easy? Those videos are out there now. They’ve already spread like wildfire. Nothing you can do to stop it."

chapters.title: Chapter 73: Pity Or Mockery
chapters.content:
Chapter 73: Pity Or Mockery

Julia’s face drained of color, her rage mingling with panic.

"You don’t understand!" she hissed, leaning closer, her voice shaking. "This could ruin me. My career. My reputation. You were supposed to be on my side!"

The man met her desperate gaze with a cold, indifferent stare.

"On your side?" he repeated, his voice dripping with amusement. "Lady, I did the job you paid me for. Everything else? Well, that’s on you."

Julia’s heart raced as her mind spun, trying to process the reality of the situation.

She thought she had everything under control, but now, everything was falling apart—and this man, the very one she had hired, was at the center of it.

"Why?" she asked, her voice quieter now but no less venomous. "Why betray me like this?"

The man leaned forward slightly, his eyes dark and unreadable. "Because," he said, "in this business, people like you? You’re just a paycheck. Nothing more. If a better deal comes along..." He trailed off, shrugging again as if it were the simplest truth in the world.

Julia trembled with anger, but beneath that was fear—fear that she had lost control of everything.

Without another word, she grabbed her bag and stormed out of the club, her mind already racing for a way to salvage what was left of her life.

---

A few days later, Julia sat in an office chair, her face buried in her hands.

The video had been circulating for days, and despite her desperate attempts to contain the damage, nothing had worked.

She had poured over a million dollars into hackers and cybersecurity specialists, hoping they could erase the scandal from social media, but all had come back with the same grim report: the person responsible for leaking the video was too powerful, and their efforts had been fruitless.

Now, Julia faced an even bigger problem.

The sound of fingers tapping on the table pulled her from her thoughts.

Amos, Lucas’s business advisor and assistant, sat across from her, his expression stoic as always.

"Julia Mayhem," Amos called, his voice calm but firm.

Julia lifted her head, her eyes tired and ringed with dark circles from sleepless nights.

Seated beside Amos was Lucas, who watched her with a mixture of concern and frustration.

He noticed how worn down she looked, but he knew this meeting was unavoidable.

His company, the Rossi’s entertainment company, had already taken a hit from the fallout of the video—small losses, but the risk of greater damage loomed ahead if they didn’t act fast.

"Julia," Lucas began, choosing his words carefully, "I’m really sorry about all of this. But the reality is, I have to put my business first. The company’s reputation is on the line, and we’re already seeing the effects. If this continues..." He paused, the weight of his words clear without needing further explanation.

Julia’s lips quivered, her eyes welling with tears.

She adjusted the cap she wore low over her face, her hair tied back in a bun, trying to maintain whatever dignity she had left.

"I understand," she whispered, her voice barely audible as she glanced at Lucas.

Her shame was evident, though she fought hard to contain it.

Lucas gave a small nod before signaling to Amos, who pulled out a folder from his briefcase and placed it in front of Julia.

The document inside was marked "Contract Termination Agreement."

Amos slid a pen across the table toward her.

Julia’s heart sank as she looked at the title of the document.

The bitter taste of failure settled in her throat as she picked up the pen with a trembling hand.

Signing the agreement meant severing her business ties with Lucas, but given the scandal and the losses Rossi Entertainment had faced, it was the only logical choice.

She knew it. He knew it.

With a shaky breath, Julia scribbled her signature on the document.

Lucas leaned back in his chair, his expression unreadable as he watched her sign.

"We’ll keep the original document, and Amos will send you a copy for your records," he explained, his tone firm but lacking any malice.

He had always valued their friendship, but things had spiraled too far out of control.

Julia nodded, her throat tight.

She swallowed hard and stood, avoiding eye contact with both men.

"Thank you... for being a good partner," she managed, her voice thick with emotion.

Lucas didn’t reply immediately.

He just gave her a curt nod, watching her walk out of the office, her head low, the weight of her downfall clinging to her as she disappeared through the door.

After leaving Lucas’s office, Julia wandered into a nearby bar, her mind clouded with frustration and shame.

She sat in a corner booth, drowning her sorrows in one drink after another.

The mocking whispers and glances from people nearby did nothing to stop her.

She barely noticed them, but their words reached her ears, further fueling her need to numb herself.

As she took another sip, she heard footsteps approaching.

She blinked, her vision hazy, and looked to the side, where she saw a pair of long legs in tailored suit pants.

She slowly lifted her gaze, her eyes blurry from the alcohol.

Standing before her was a man in a sharp suit, his face unclear through her drunken haze.

The man looked down at her, amusement flickering across his features.

"Even the man you got involved with isn’t interested in helping you," he said, his voice laced with mockery.

Julia felt a wave of annoyance wash over her.

Who was this man? And why was he here, trying to humiliate her like everyone else?

She didn’t care who he was; she just hated him for speaking to her like that.

The man continued, "Ben managed to blur his face from the videos. He kept his reputation intact. Meanwhile, here you are."

His tone shifted slightly, though still sharp. "Honestly, I feel pity."

Julia raised an eyebrow, her mind sluggish but catching onto his words.

"Pity or mockery?" she asked, her voice slurred but defiant.

A smirk crept across the man’s thin lips.

"Honestly? It’s both," he replied coolly.

He tucked his hands into his pockets, his expression unreadable. "But I can help you."

Julia’s drunken haze faltered for a moment. Her heart skipped a beat.

She squinted at the man, trying to discern whether he was being serious or just toying with her.

"Help me?" she echoed, disbelief in her voice.

No one had offered to help her—not the people she had helped rise in the industry, not her so-called friends.

And now, here was a stranger, offering a lifeline.

The man pulled a business card from his pocket and dropped it onto the table.

"Contact me, and we’ll discuss how I can help you get that video removed. Maybe even help you get back on your feet," he said, his tone almost casual, as if this were a routine transaction.

Julia stared at the card, unsure if this was some sort of trick.

She wanted to grab it, but doubt gnawed at her.

Why would a stranger want to help her when even those closest to her had abandoned her?

The man turned and walked away without waiting for her response, leaving Julia sitting there with the card in front of her.

She reached out slowly, picking it up, her mind racing despite the alcohol dulling her senses.

This could be her chance.

But could she trust him?

Julia’s grip tightened on the card as she watched the man leave, her thoughts swirling with the faint hope that maybe, just maybe, this stranger could help her regain what she had lost.

chapters.title: Chapter 74: Drunk Woman
chapters.content:
Chapter 74: Drunk Woman

Aurora stood in her bedroom, the calming sound of rain pattering against the windows as she finished applying moisturizing cream to her skin.

Her V-neck nightgown, soft and flattering, hugged her gently, especially around her growing belly.

She was ready to slip into bed when a knock echoed on her door.

"Come in," she called out softly.

The door creaked open, and Ava stepped in, her expression slightly troubled. "Sorry if I’m interrupting your rest."

Aurora shook her head, offering a small smile. "Oh no, you’re not."

Ava nodded, relieved. "I just wanted to inform you... the security man told me there’s a drunk woman outside the mansion. She’s refusing to leave."

Aurora raised an eyebrow, curious. "A drunk woman?"

"Yes," Ava confirmed, walking toward the window. "You can see her from here."

Aurora slipped her soft slippers onto her feet and joined Ava at the window.

As she peered through the rain-streaked glass, she saw her—Julia, kneeling in the pouring rain.

Her hair clung to her face as the rain battered down on her, and then, to Aurora’s surprise, Julia began to scream.

"How could you do this to me?" Julia’s voice cracked through the night air. "You’re an actress too! You know how it feels! Watching everything I built crumble..."

Aurora narrowed her eyes.

She muttered to herself, "Julia still hasn’t learned a lesson."

Her voice was barely audible, but Ava caught it.

Julia’s cries turned to pleading. "Please! Whoever’s responsible, stop the video!"

Julia didn’t seem to know if anyone was listening, but in her drunken state, she was desperate, clinging to hope.

Ava, watching the scene unfold, looked at Julia with pity.

Even Aurora felt a flash of something, a soft emotion stirring in her chest.

Julia was drenched and miserable, her sobs blending with the sound of the heavy rain.

But Aurora, after a brief moment, turned away from the window, her loose hair cascading down her back in an effortless, relaxed style.

"Ava," Aurora said firmly, "tell the security man to leave her be."

Ava blinked, surprised by her boss’s decision. "Ma’am?" she hesitated.

Aurora’s voice remained calm, though her gaze stayed fixed away from the window. "Once she’s had enough of the rain, she’ll leave on her own. Let her carry on with her act."

Ava opened her mouth, wanting to protest—Julia was clearly drunk, after all—but she swallowed her words.

She gave a respectful nod.

"Yes, ma’am," she said quietly before turning to leave the room.

Once Ava was gone, Aurora glanced back through the window.

Julia was still out there, sobbing pitifully.

A faint twinge of pity flickered in Aurora’s chest again, but she quickly pushed it aside.

"She’s pretending," Aurora whispered to herself.

After a moment, Aurora picked up her phone.

Her fingers tapped the screen, and soon she was dialing the number of the man she had contacted before—the leader of the rugged men.

The call connected after a few rings.

"Mrs. Aurora," the man’s voice came through, steady and confident.

"Stop the video," Aurora said without hesitation.

The man paused. "But ma’am, this... this is nothing compared to what you wanted her to feel."

Aurora let out a slow breath. "I know. But it’s enough. Take it down. Now."

"As you wish, Mrs. Aurora," the man replied, sounding slightly surprised.

Aurora ended the call, letting her phone fall softly onto the bed.

She stared out of the window again, her mind wandering.

She didn’t even notice when Julia had left or when someone else quietly entered her room—until she felt it.

Warm hands gently wrapped around her waist, followed by a soft kiss pressed against her neck.

Aurora gasped, caught off guard by the gesture.

She quickly turned and saw Lucas standing behind her.

He looked as handsome as ever, even though his tailored suit was slightly rumpled from what must have been a long day at the office.

His white shirt remained sharp despite his loosened tie, a hint of relaxation in his appearance.

His perfectly styled hair was slightly tousled, and his warm smile lit up his features.

"You startled me," Aurora said, her heartbeat slowly settling.

Lucas chuckled, his eyes sparkling with affection. "I wanted to surprise you," he admitted, his voice soft. "Sorry for coming without calling first."

Aurora shook her head, trying to lighten her tone. "You didn’t scare me."

But Lucas’s eyes narrowed slightly. "Are you sure?" he asked. "You seem... off."

Aurora was about to brush it off again, but Lucas gently tilted her chin upward, his eyes searching hers.

He paused, his expression shifting when he noticed the tears welling up in her eyes.

"Aurora..." Lucas’s voice was filled with concern as she leaned into him, resting her head against his chest.

He stiffened slightly, startled by her sudden vulnerability.

Aurora’s emotions spilled over, and she started to cry quietly into his chest.

Lucas, clearly taken aback, stepped back just enough to look at her face.

"What’s wrong?" he asked urgently. "Did someone hurt you? Tell me who it is, and I’ll make them pay."

Aurora shook her head, wiping her tears with trembling fingers. "No, it’s not that," she whispered. "It’s about... our pups."

Lucas’s brow furrowed in confusion. "What do you mean?"

Aurora let out a shaky breath, turning her face away. "I don’t think I can continue anymore."

Lucas was stunned. "What?" he said softly, disbelief in his voice.

She looked down, her hands trembling slightly. "I know it’s disappointing, but... I don’t think I can do this."

For a moment, Lucas was speechless.

He could still see the worry and fear in her eyes.

Without a word, he gently led her to the bed, helping her sit down carefully before pulling up a chair to sit across from her.

He took her hand in his.

"Alright," Lucas said softly, his voice soothing. "I’m listening. What’s going on?"

Aurora bit her lip, her gaze falling to the floor. "I don’t want the pups anymore."

Her words hung in the air, the quiet rain outside the only sound filling the room.

chapters.title: Chapter 75: Pre-delivery Rituals
chapters.content:
Chapter 75: Pre-delivery Rituals

Lucas remained steady, his gaze unwavering as he watched Aurora.

"Why?" His voice was calm but firm. "Is something bothering you? Is it about me? Did I do something that made you change your mind?"

Aurora said nothing, her eyes still fixed on the floor.

Lucas waited, reading the silence between them. "Alright," he said, his tone steady but gentle. "If you don’t want to talk about it now, we can talk later. Whenever you’re ready."

He tried to keep his voice calm, but inside, questions nagged at him. Was this his fault? Had his past mistakes pushed her away? Maybe she didn’t want anything to do with him or their pups.

Aurora must have sensed his thoughts because she straightened, letting out a breath.

"It’s not that," she said firmly. "We should talk about it now."

Lucas nodded, his attention fully on her.

"As you know," Aurora began, her voice clear, "the delivery date is getting closer."

"Yeah," Lucas replied.

"I’m anxious," she admitted.

Lucas’s thoughts flashed to their recent discussion with Dr. Evans. They had decided on a natural childbirth. Was she second-guessing it?

"It’s okay," he said quickly, reaching for her hand. "If you’re nervous about the delivery, we can switch to a C-section. Whatever makes you comfortable."

Aurora shook her head. "No, it’s not that."

Lucas furrowed his brow. "Then what is it?"

"It’s about the pre-delivery rituals my pack performs," Aurora said, her tone gaining strength.

Lucas leaned forward, intrigued. "Rituals?"

"It’s part of the tradition," she explained. "The mother goes back to her pack before the pups are born to complete the ceremony."

Lucas nodded slowly. "Alright, so we go to your pack. I’ll come with you."

Aurora’s grip tightened on his hand. "It’s not that simple."

"Why not? I’ll go with you, whenever you’re ready."

Aurora met his eyes, her voice lowering. "If I go back, my father might try to kill me."

Lucas’s eyes widened. "What? Why would he—"

"My father despises the Rossi family," Aurora said bluntly. "The Grey and Rossi families have always hated each other. If he finds out I’m pregnant with your pups again... he might kill us all."

Lucas fell silent, the weight of her words sinking in. He hadn’t realized how deep the feud between their families ran. The idea of Aurora facing danger because of their bond hit him hard.

But Lucas wasn’t one to back down. He offered her a small, reassuring smile. "It’s okay. I’ll still go with you. And maybe I’ll get to see your mother while we’re there."

Aurora couldn’t help the smile that crept onto her face. His steady presence, his refusal to shy away from the danger, lightened the tension she’d been carrying.

Lucas squeezed her hand. "Is that why you’ve been feeling like this?" he asked. "Is that why you don’t want the pups?"

Aurora nodded slowly, the vulnerability still in her eyes.

Lucas leaned closer, his voice resolute. "Aurora, we’ll get through this. I’ve been thinking about taking time off work. We still need to plan the nursery, pick names, do all the things parents do before the babies arrive. And I want to be there for you, every step of the way."

Aurora’s smile grew, her heart lighter. Lucas had changed. He was more present, more dedicated. She could feel it.

Leaning in, Lucas kissed her forehead. "Let’s get you to bed," he said softly. "We’ll figure the rest out together."

Aurora nodded, feeling at peace for the first time in a while.

---

Ava drove them to the Grey Pack, the tension evident as they neared the imposing gates.

She had insisted on accompanying Aurora, ready to assist her if necessary.

When the car rolled to a stop, the guards quickly marched out, one of them approaching the driver’s side.

Ava rolled down the window, revealing her face. The guard nodded, recognizing her, and was about to wave them through when his eyes caught sight of Lucas in the back seat with Aurora.

His expression shifted.

Silently, the guard made a call, and soon, several others surrounded the car.

Aurora felt her pulse quicken, but Lucas gently squeezed her hand, his voice calm as he leaned toward her.

"Stay calm," he whispered.

Aurora took a steadying breath before speaking to the guard. "Lucas is with me. You should call my father to confirm our entry."

The guard hesitated before nodding and placing a call.

A moment later, after a curt conversation, the guards backed away, and the gate creaked open.

Ava felt a chill creep down her spine as she drove through, her gaze fixed on the looming mansion ahead.

Something about the place felt dark, unwelcoming.

She stopped the car in front of the entrance, her hands gripping the steering wheel a little too tightly.

"You two go ahead," Ava said with a small smile that didn’t quite reach her eyes. "I’ll stay in the car."

Aurora noticed her discomfort but nodded. "Alright. We won’t be long."

She stepped out with Lucas by her side, her hand slipping around his arm for support as they approached the mansion.

The head maid was already waiting, guiding them inside and through the corridors until they reached the grand dining hall.

At the head of the table sat Alpha Grey, his presence suffocating the room with authority.

On either side of him were his wives: Victoria, Aurora’s mother, and Elizabeth, the stepmother.

Victoria’s eyes lit up when she saw Aurora, her body instinctively moving to rise, but she caught herself.

"Aurora," Victoria breathed, her voice full of relief.

She quickly crossed the room and enveloped her daughter in a warm embrace.

Aurora returned the hug briefly, grateful for the familiar comfort.

Victoria’s gaze then shifted to Lucas, standing silently beside her daughter. Her body tensed slightly, and she quickly looked back at her husband, who was glaring daggers at Lucas.

Alpha Grey’s dark eyes burned with fury as he surveyed the scene.

His grip on the armrests of his chair tightened, but he said nothing as Victoria, now visibly nervous, stepped back from Aurora and resumed her seat.

Lucas, ever composed, bowed his head in respect. "Alpha Grey," he greeted, his voice steady.

Alpha Grey scoffed, leaning back in his chair. "What brings a Rossi to my pack?" His voice dripped with disdain.

chapters.title: Chapter 76: Bewitched
chapters.content:
Chapter 76: Bewitched

Lucas met Alpha Grey’s gaze without flinching. "I’ve come with my wife, Aurora, for the pre-delivery ritual."

The room fell silent.

Alpha Grey’s laugh started low, then grew louder, a sharp, mocking sound that echoed off the stone walls.

"Pre-delivery ritual?" he repeated, as if the words were a joke. "I thought you two were divorced."

Aurora’s hand instinctively went to her belly, a protective gesture that drew Alpha Grey’s attention.

His laughter died instantly, replaced by a deadly calm as he focused on her.

"We’re not divorced," Aurora said, her voice firm despite the fear that prickled at the back of her mind. "And I’m carrying Lucas’s pups."

Victoria gasped, her eyes wide as she finally noticed the subtle swell beneath Aurora’s dress.

"Again?" she blurted out, the shock clear in her voice.

Across the table, Elizabeth, who had remained silent through it all, sipped her wine with a slight smirk, as though she enjoyed watching the chaos unfold.

Lucas stepped forward, his posture confident. "Aurora is my wife, and we’re expecting our pups. I’m here with her this time, fully supporting her through everything."

Alpha Grey’s face twisted with rage. In an instant, he pulled out a gun from his belt, standing up so abruptly his chair clattered to the floor.

The room froze.

Aurora’s heart raced, but Lucas didn’t hesitate.

He moved in front of her, shielding her with his body.

"Grey, stop!" Victoria’s voice trembled as she shot up from her seat, her hands raised in a plea. "Please, just calm down."

But Alpha Grey was beyond reason. His gaze never left Lucas as he took slow, deliberate steps toward them, the gun still aimed.

"You fool," he spat at Aurora. "I thought you’d finally come to your senses. But now I see this man has bewitched you."

"I haven’t bewitched her," Lucas said, his voice calm but commanding.

Alpha Grey’s glare intensified. "Shut up and move aside. I should’ve eliminated her and those cursed pups the moment I knew."

Aurora trembled behind Lucas, her nails digging into his arm as she clung to him for strength.

She couldn’t believe the words coming out of her father’s mouth, yet they didn’t surprise her. Not really.

Victoria, desperate now, stepped forward. "Grey, please. Let’s talk about this. They came here peacefully."

"No!" Alpha Grey roared, turning his rage on his wife. "This is your fault. If you had raised our daughter properly, none of this would be happening."

Victoria recoiled as though she’d been slapped, but she didn’t back down. "She’s our daughter, Grey. And she’s pregnant. She needs our support."

Alpha Grey sneered. "Support? For this? For him?" He gestured violently toward Lucas with the gun. "I will not let this filth stain our bloodline any further."

Lucas remained unshaken, his voice steady as ever. "Aurora’s not alone in this. I’ll protect her and our pups, no matter what."

Aurora, though still terrified, drew on Lucas’s strength.

She stepped out from behind him, her eyes locking onto her father’s.

"I made my choice, Father," she said, her voice fierce despite the situation. "Lucas is my mate, and we’re having these pups. Whether you accept it or not, we’re family."

For a moment, Alpha Grey’s eyes flickered, as though he hadn’t expected such defiance from her.

But the fury quickly returned, and he raised the gun again, his finger on the trigger.

Victoria, on the verge of panic, called out again. "Grey, stop! If you don’t—"

Before she could finish, Lucas took a step forward, positioning himself directly between Aurora and the gun. "You’ll have to go through me first."

Alpha Grey’s grip on the gun remained firm, and the cold click of the trigger echoed in the room.

Aurora’s breath caught, her heart stopping for a split second as the gun fired.

But in that moment, Victoria leapt forward, kicking at the weapon with all her strength.

The bullet whizzed past Lucas, striking the wall behind him with a sharp thud.

Aurora gasped, her hands flying to her mouth.

Lucas, unfazed, turned to ensure she was unharmed.

Meanwhile, Alpha Grey snarled in frustration, glaring at his wife.

"What are you doing, woman?" Alpha Grey spat, his voice thick with rage. "Do you want to die too?"

Victoria didn’t back down. "Enough of this, Grey! You’re out of your mind!"

She stepped between Alpha Grey and Lucas, her body shaking but her voice unwavering. "They are your daughter and her husband. Have you forgotten what family means?"

Alpha Grey’s face twisted with fury. "Family? This man is no family of mine!"

Elizabeth, who had been watching with an amused smirk, finally spoke. "Oh, Victoria, you’ve always been too soft. Let Grey handle this."

Aurora saw her chance as the argument heated up.

While Alpha Grey and Victoria clashed, and Elizabeth stirred the pot with her cruel remarks, Aurora tugged on Lucas’s arm, whispering, "Come with me."

She quietly led Lucas out of the room, glancing back to ensure they weren’t being followed.

The argument raged on behind them, loud and heated enough to drown out the soft click of the door closing as they slipped away.

Aurora guided Lucas down a dim corridor, her steps hurried and uneven.

As they entered a small, secluded room, Aurora swiftly locked the door behind them.

She pressed her back against it, finally letting out the breath she hadn’t realized she was holding.

The room was quiet, a stark contrast to the chaos they had just escaped.

"Did he hurt you?" Aurora asked, her voice strained with concern as she looked Lucas over.

"No," Lucas replied gently, stepping closer to her. "I’m fine, Aurora. Don’t worry."

Aurora’s shoulders sagged with exhaustion.

"We need to leave," she said, her voice firm though her hands trembled. "We can’t stay here any longer."

Lucas’s brow furrowed. "But what about the rituals? If we don’t perform them, there will be consequences. The witches could come for our pups—"

"I don’t care about the rituals!" Aurora snapped, running her hands through her hair. "We’ll protect our pups ourselves if it comes to that. I’m not staying here any longer."

Lucas shook his head. "It’s too dangerous to ignore the rituals, Aurora. You know what’s at stake."

She let out a frustrated sigh, collapsing onto the bed. "I just don’t know what to do anymore, Lucas," she whispered, her voice heavy with weariness.

Lucas sat beside her, placing a comforting hand on her shoulder. "We’ll figure this out together. We always do."

Aurora closed her eyes, letting the exhaustion take over, but her sleep was restless.

The weight of the situation gnawed at her, and even in sleep, she couldn’t escape the overwhelming sense of danger surrounding them.

---

When Aurora’s eyes snapped open again, sunlight was streaming through the window.

She blinked, disoriented, before realizing Lucas was no longer in the room.

Panic surged through her chest.

"Lucas?" she called, her voice tight as she rushed out of bed.

She searched the room, finding no sign of him. Her mind raced with worst-case scenarios, her father’s violent outburst still fresh in her memory.

Hastily, she threw on her shoes and bolted out the door, moving quickly down the hallway.

She passed a maid dusting a cabinet and stopped abruptly.

"Have you seen Lucas?" Aurora asked, her voice breathless.

The maid nodded. "He’s outside, at the other side of the house, Miss."

Aurora’s heart pounded as she made her way outside, her feet barely touching the ground as she hurried around the mansion.

The sound of laughter—loud, carefree—stopped her in her tracks.

She followed the sound until she reached the back of the estate.

There, she froze.

Lucas was standing beside Alpha Grey, both of them holding golf clubs, grinning at each other like old friends.

Alpha Grey swung his club, hitting the ball with surprising ease, and Lucas clapped him on the shoulder, laughing at something her father said.

Aurora’s mouth fell open.

What is happening?

Lucas, sensing her presence, turned around and smiled.

"Here she is!" he called, as though he had been waiting for her all along.

Alpha Grey turned as well, and to Aurora’s shock, he was smiling too.

"Morning, Aurora," Alpha Grey said, his voice unusually light.

The warmth in his tone was jarring, completely unlike the rage-fueled Alpha from last night.

Aurora’s heart raced, confusion swirling in her mind. She had expected to find chaos or violence—anything but this strange scene of unity.

chapters.title: Chapter 77: Slow Sip
chapters.content:
Chapter 77: Slow Sip

As Aurora joined them, Lucas moved closer, pressing a soft kiss on her forehead.

Alpha Grey’s calm demeanor made her frown in confusion.

She leaned toward Lucas, whispering, "What’s going on?"

Lucas chuckled lightly, his confidence unshaken. "I told you, we’d figure it out. Alpha Grey and I had an amicable chat."

Aurora crossed her arms, unconvinced. "My father doesn’t do ’amicable.’ He doesn’t talk to anyone he dislikes without—well, without leaving a mark. Chaos is more his style."

"True," Lucas admitted with a wry smile. "But I spoke to him like a son would. Showed him some love."

Aurora raised an eyebrow, skeptical of his smooth explanation. That look made Lucas sigh. "Fine. We had a bit of a... disagreement. A proper wolf fight. Claws, teeth, you name it. Even Victoria and Elizabeth couldn’t step in. But I managed to get through to him."

Her eyes widened. "And how exactly did you ’get through to him’?"

Lucas glanced away before answering, "I may have offered him a sizable piece of land from the Rossi pack."

Aurora gasped. "You what?"

"Relax, it worked." Lucas grinned, glancing over at Alpha Grey, who was now contently focusing on his golf game. "And now he’s happy to move forward with the pre-delivery rituals."

Aurora sighed, relieved yet concerned. "Are you sure this won’t come back to hurt you?"

"Maybe a little," Lucas shrugged. "But nothing I can’t handle."

Aurora studied him for a moment before exhaling. "Fine. If we’re doing the ritual, let’s do it now."

Lucas smiled, taking her hand. "Whenever you’re ready, I’m ready."

--- Several Weeks Later ---

Aurora gripped the hospital bed sheets, her breath coming in ragged gasps. Sweat beaded her forehead as the nurse beside her encouraged her to push.

"Come on, Aurora, you’re doing great. Just a little more. Push!" the nurse urged, her voice steady, but Aurora was struggling.

"I can’t," Aurora panted, her face pale. "I need Lucas. Call him, please."

The nurse nodded quickly, pulling out her phone. "I’ll call him right now."

Aurora closed her eyes, trying to steady her breath as another contraction gripped her.

The nurse dialed Lucas’s number, but after a few moments, her expression faltered.

The phone rang and rang with no answer.

Aurora opened her eyes, her voice trembling. "Did you get him?"

The nurse hesitated, signaling quietly to the other nurse in the room.

Aurora’s eyes darted between them. "What’s happening? Isn’t he answering?"

The second nurse stepped forward, her smile kind but clearly forced. "He’ll come, Aurora. Don’t worry. But right now, we need you to focus on delivering these babies."

Aurora’s frustration spiked. "That bastard," she muttered under her breath, about to launch into a tirade when another contraction hit her hard.

She groaned, bearing down with all her strength.

"Push, Aurora, push!" the first nurse encouraged.

Aurora let out a loud cry, her body trembling as she gave one last effort.

Moments later, the first baby arrived with a sharp wail.

"It’s a boy!" the nurse exclaimed, holding up the newborn before wrapping him in a soft blanket.

The baby’s cries filled the room, and despite the pain, Aurora’s eyes brimmed with tears as she watched her son. But the labor wasn’t over yet.

"One more, Aurora. You’re almost there," the nurse said, preparing her for the second push.

Aurora nodded, gathering the last of her strength. With another deep breath, she pushed again, harder this time. A few moments later, the second baby arrived, letting out a softer but distinct cry.

"A girl!" the nurse smiled, wrapping the tiny baby in another blanket.

Aurora felt a surge of relief as she lay back against the pillows, utterly exhausted but filled with joy. The sound of her babies’ cries was the sweetest thing she’d ever heard.

"They’re beautiful," Aurora whispered, her chest rising and falling as she tried to catch her breath.

The nurses quickly began the newborn examinations, cooing over the little ones. "They’re perfect, healthy babies," one of the nurses said, smiling down at Aurora.

Aurora’s eyes fluttered shut, her exhaustion overwhelming her. "Thank you," she murmured, her voice barely above a whisper.

The last thing she heard before she drifted off was the soft cries of her newborns, their tiny voices filling the room as she succumbed to a much-needed rest.

---

Meanwhile, in the backseat of a luxurious car, Lucas sat anxiously, glancing out the window before turning to Amos. "Amos, can’t you drive any faster? I need to be there with Aurora."

Amos pressed on the gas, speeding up slightly but not too much. "I’m going as fast as I can, sir. The roads are a bit tricky tonight."

Lucas let out a frustrated breath, tapping his fingers on his leg. "I was supposed to be there already. That meeting with Alpha Grey—he insisted it was mandatory." He shook his head. "Aurora must be worried sick."

Reaching into his pocket out of habit, Lucas suddenly froze. "My phone!" he exclaimed. "I left it back at the meeting."

Amos glanced at him through the rearview mirror. "Do you want me to turn back and get it?"

"No," Lucas replied quickly. "We don’t have time. Keep going."

Just as he was about to say something else, Lucas glanced up and his eyes went wide.

A truck was barreling straight toward them.

"Amos, watch out!" Lucas shouted, panic in his voice.

Amos reacted instantly, yanking the steering wheel to the side.

The car swerved sharply, but it was too late.

The vehicle skidded off the road, tipping and tumbling before crashing onto its side, the world spinning around them.

The car came to a halt, lying overturned, while the truck rolled to a stop nearby.

The truck driver climbed out, his boots crunching against the gravel as he made his way toward the wrecked car.

His smirk deepened as he saw the vehicle leaking fuel onto the ground.

Peering through the shattered window, the driver bent down, placing a hand over Lucas’s nose.

After a few seconds, he pulled back, satisfied. "Not breathing," he muttered to himself, grinning in triumph.

Taking out his phone, the man dialed a number. The call connected quickly.

"Alpha Grey," the truck driver said, his voice low, "the Rossi is dead, just like you wanted."

On the other end, Alpha Grey’s lips curled into a cold smile. "Good."

Without another word, he ended the call and took a slow sip of his wine, the glass gleaming in the dim light.

chapters.title: Chapter 78: Missing Baby
chapters.content:
Chapter 78: Missing Baby

Aurora slowly opened her eyes, blinking away the grogginess.

As her vision cleared, she noticed Ava sitting beside her.

Ava’s face lit up with a gentle smile.

"You’re awake," Ava said softly.

Aurora nodded and slowly sat up, her body still aching from the delivery.

Ava quickly moved to assist her, adjusting the pillows behind Aurora’s back for support.

"Thank you," Aurora murmured.

Ava then carefully lifted the baby girl from the crib nearby, placing the tiny bundle into Aurora’s arms.

"She’s beautiful," Ava whispered, her eyes softening. "She looks just like Sir Lucas."

Aurora’s gaze fell upon her daughter, and her heart melted.

The baby had delicate features, her cheeks rosy and soft, with a tiny tuft of dark hair atop her head.

But it was the baby’s eyes that caught Aurora’s breath—those piercing blue eyes, the same vivid blue that Lucas had.

Aurora’s lips trembled with emotion as she cradled her daughter closer.

"My Aria," she whispered, the name she had chosen for her daughter falling softly from her lips.

Ava smiled warmly. "Aria suits her perfectly. She’s absolutely adorable."

Aurora, still gazing at her daughter, suddenly remembered Lucas.

The blue eyes brought his absence crashing back into her mind.

She looked up at Ava, her expression shifting.

"Did you reach Lucas?" Aurora asked, a hint of frustration lacing her voice.

Ava hesitated. "No... I couldn’t reach him at all."

Aurora frowned, her annoyance growing. "The nurses said he would be here soon, but this isn’t what we planned. Lucas promised he’d be by my side, not missing like this."

Her voice wavered slightly, but her frustration remained. "Where is he?"

Ava placed a hand on her shoulder, trying to soothe her. "I’m sure something came up. You know Sir Lucas—he’ll be here as soon as he can."

Aurora sighed, her heart heavy. "It’s fine," she muttered, glancing down at Aria, who was resting peacefully in her arms. "What about Leo?"

Ava blinked in confusion. "Leo?"

"Yes," Aurora said, her voice firm. "My son. I had a boy first."

Ava’s brow furrowed. "I... I think you should speak to the doctor. I’ll go get him."

Ava quickly exited the room, leaving Aurora with a gnawing sense that something was terribly wrong.

A few minutes later, Dr. Evans entered the room with Ava beside him.

He gave Aurora a calm smile, though his expression was serious. "Aurora, I understand you’re asking about a second child?"

Aurora nodded, her eyes narrowing slightly. "Yes. I had my son first, then Aria."

Dr. Evans cleared his throat gently. "I’m afraid there’s been a misunderstanding. There was an ultrasound error—there was only one baby. You delivered a healthy girl, and that’s it."

Aurora stared at him, her voice rising. "What? No! I had Leo first, then Aria. I remember the nurses clearly saying, ’It’s a boy,’ then ’It’s a girl.’"

Dr. Evans shook his head. "I assure you, nothing like that happened. You only delivered a girl."

"Call the nurse!" Aurora demanded, her frustration boiling over. "I want her to confirm what I remember."

Dr. Evans nodded, signaling to the nurse, who arrived shortly after.

The nurse confirmed what Dr. Evans had said. "It was only one child, a baby girl," the nurse stated.

Aurora’s head began to pound as a sharp headache struck her.

She pressed her hand to her forehead, feeling exhaustion creep in, but she pushed it aside.

"No," she muttered. "I know what I remember. Leo was born first."

Dr. Evans placed a hand on her chart. "Aurora, I think it’s best if you rest. Ava will take care of everything."

Aurora shook her head.

"No," she said firmly. "I don’t have time to rest when my baby is missing."

Ava looked at her, concerned, but Aurora was resolute.

She turned to Dr. Evans. "I want access to the hospital’s CCTV footage. I want to see what happened during my delivery and in this ward."

Dr. Evans paled slightly. "I’m afraid that’s against hospital policy. We don’t release CCTV footage to patients."

Aurora’s gaze hardened, her voice dropping to a dangerous tone. "Then I suggest you speak to the administration. Let’s see if they’ll really deny me access when I’m trying to find my missing child."

Dr. Evans gulped, visibly nervous. "O-Okay, I’ll... I’ll speak to them. Please, just give me a moment."

He hurried out of the room, clearly unsettled.

Later, Aurora was led to the hospital’s security office with Dr. Evans.

Ava stayed behind in the ward with Aria, as Aurora trusted her to take care of her daughter.

Ava had proven to be a reliable support in the past, and despite initially declining Ava’s offer to be the baby’s sitter, Aurora had eventually agreed.

Ava had managed both roles perfectly, and Aurora knew she could trust her with Aria.

In the security office, the footage played, showing Aurora’s delivery.

As the cry of the first baby echoed through the room, Aurora leaned forward. "That’s exactly how I remembered it. My boy came first."

The footage continued, and the cry of the second baby followed.

Aurora’s eyes narrowed.

Then, the screen showed a nurse walking out of the ward with a baby wrapped in a blanket, her steps hurried and suspicious.

Aurora’s heart pounded in her chest as she pointed to the screen. "That’s him! My son! That nurse took him!"

Dr. Evans stared at the footage in shock. "That nurse..."

He leaned in closer. "I don’t recognize her. She’s not from our hospital."

Aurora turned to him, her eyes blazing with fury. "So much for your hospital policy. Your nurse kidnapped my son!"

Dr. Evans swallowed hard, clearly rattled. "I swear, I’ve never seen her before."

The footage continued, showing the mysterious nurse exiting the hospital and placing the baby into a car before driving off.

Aurora’s jaw clenched as she took out her phone, her fingers trembling with anger.

She dialed a number, and within moments, the call was answered.

"Derek," she said, her voice steady and firm, "I need you to help me track down a car with the following plate number: ZXT 9481. I want the location immediately."

Without waiting for a response, Aurora leaned back in the chair, her expression cold and resolute.

She wasn’t going to rest until she found her son.

chapters.title: Chapter 79: Gurgling Sounds
chapters.content:
Chapter 79: Gurgling Sounds

Aurora’s phone rang, cutting through the heavy silence of the hospital room.

It was Derek.

She answered swiftly, her voice sharp with anticipation.

"Did you find the car?" Aurora asked, already standing and gathering her things to leave.

"I tracked the plate number," Derek replied, his voice steady. "The car’s current location is within Grey Pack territory... precisely at Alpha Grey’s mansion."

Aurora froze, her hand tightening around the phone.

"What?" Her mind raced.

Alpha Grey? Her heart pounded harder, but she forced herself to stay calm.

"Yes," Derek confirmed. "That’s where it’s parked now."

Aurora’s grip on the phone faltered slightly, her thoughts tumbling over one another. She glanced back at the security screen, still paused on the footage.

"I’ll handle it from here, Derek." She ended the call abruptly, her focus now razor sharp.

Turning to the security officer, she pointed at the screen. "Replay the footage where the baby was taken to the car."

The officer nodded and rewound the footage, replaying the moment when the suspicious nurse walked out of the hospital carrying her son.

Aurora leaned in, her eyes scanning every detail on the screen.

"Zoom in on the driver," she commanded, her voice steady but cold.

The security officer zoomed in as requested, and the blurred image of the driver slowly sharpened.

The face in the car came into focus, and Aurora’s eyes widened in disbelief.

Her breath caught in her throat.

It was unmistakable—the man behind the wheel was one of her father’s guards.

Her jaw dropped as recognition hit her like a punch to the gut. She stood motionless, staring at the screen, her mind now connecting fragments she hadn’t considered before.

"What is one of my father’s guards doing involved in this?"

---

Aurora gripped the wheel tightly as she drove into the heart of the Grey Pack.

Derek’s intel had led her to this sacred place, one few dared to enter.

The moon cast an eerie glow over the surroundings, but Aurora felt no fear—only determination.

She pulled the car to a halt and stepped out, her gaze cold and focused.

Derek and his men flanked her, each armed with guns loaded with silvery bullets.

They moved silently, creeping toward the outer perimeter where the guards stood watch.

Without hesitation, Derek and his men engaged the guards.

Blows were exchanged, gunfire muffled by the silvery bullets.

Aurora didn’t linger—she pushed forward, entering the empty building ahead.

Her footsteps echoed as she searched through the desolate halls.

The silence was suffocating, her heart pounding in her chest.

She murmured a prayer to the Moon Goddess, begging for guidance.

Then, piercing the stillness, a baby’s cry echoed through the space.

Her son.

Aurora’s eyes snapped toward the sound, her heart lurching. She followed it, the cry leading her to a wall.

It was faint but unmistakable.

Without a second thought, she kicked the wall with all her strength.

The barrier crumbled under her force, revealing a hidden room beyond.

There, standing with her baby in his arms, was Alpha Grey.

He was poised over a small tub of water, and her son was inches from its surface.

Aurora knew instantly—that was her baby.

The small form, the unmistakable energy, the connection she felt to him since the moment he was born.

His cry was filled with desperation, and those tiny blue eyes were exactly like Lucas’s. There was no doubt in her mind.

"Stay away from my son!" Aurora growled, pointing her gun directly at Alpha Grey.

Alpha Grey turned slowly, a smirk tugging at the corners of his mouth. His guard stood beside him, watching with cold eyes.

Alpha Grey chuckled darkly. "Your son? Do you really think you can stop me?"

Aurora’s fingers twitched on the trigger, but before she could fire, Alpha Grey snapped his fingers.

Suddenly, men emerged from the shadows, surrounding her.

Aurora shot at the first few that charged her, taking them down with precision, but there were too many.

They rushed her, pinning her to the ground.

Silvery chains were wrapped around her neck and wrists, searing her skin like fire.

She groaned in pain, struggling to free herself.

Alpha Grey walked toward her, his smirk deepening. "Now, look at that," he taunted. "Much more submissive to your father."

Aurora scoffed, baring her fangs at him. "Why did you take my child from me?" she demanded, her voice raw with fury.

Alpha Grey looked down at the baby, then back at her.

"You mean this filth?" he said with disdain.

Aurora’s growl deepened, her fangs still bared in defiance.

Unfazed, Alpha Grey continued, "During the pre-delivery ritual, it was revealed that this son of yours is the next Alpha King, chosen by the Moon Goddess herself. He is supposed to replace me."

His eyes darkened with malice. "But that won’t happen. Just like the other pups I eliminated to keep my position, this one will be no different."

Aurora’s stomach churned as the truth sank in. Her father had been killing innocent pups, chosen by fate, to maintain his power. And now he intended to murder her son.

"You’re disgusting," she spat, her voice dripping with venom. "Despicable."

Alpha Grey merely shrugged, unaffected. He moved the baby closer to the tub.

Aurora’s heart raced, her desperation mounting.

"Don’t do it!" she screamed, her voice cracking with raw emotion. "Don’t harm my pup!"

She fought against the chains, the silver burning deeper into her skin, sending waves of agony through her body.

Her vision blurred, but she didn’t stop. She couldn’t stop.

Alpha Grey ignored her, dipping the baby into the water.

Aurora’s heart shattered as she heard the gurgling sounds. Her baby was drowning.

Rage erupted inside her, a fire unlike anything she had ever felt.

With one final, agonized cry, she pushed against the chains.

The pain was unbearable, but she channelled every ounce of strength she had left.

Then, something happened. Aurora felt a surge of power, something deep and primal. Her body shuddered, and in a violent burst of energy, she transformed into her wolf.

The silvery chains snapped like paper, falling to the ground.

Alpha Grey and his guard turned, stunned. They had never seen Aurora transform—they believed she was wolfless, weak. But here she stood, her pure white wolf towering before them, radiant with power and fury.

chapters.title: Chapter 80: Grave Expression
chapters.content:
Chapter 80: Grave Expression

Without hesitation, Aurora lunged at Alpha Grey.

Her powerful form collided with him, sending him flying through the air.

He crashed to the ground with a thud, groaning in pain.

Aurora’s gaze turned to her son, her wolf’s eyes softening for a brief moment.

She would protect him—no matter what.

Alpha Grey groaned, pushing himself up from the ground.

His eyes glowed with rage as he transformed into his wolf—a massive beast, towering over Aurora. His dark fur bristled, and his growl reverberated through the room like thunder.

Aurora’s breath hitched for a moment.

This was her first time in wolf form, her first time facing someone so powerful.
